steps.title: Identify the critical prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that leaders reflecting on mistakes with learning should show more humility than those without this reflection. The supporting data would show the learning-based group scoring highest on the humility scale.

steps.title: Find the group with the highest humility score
steps.content:
From the graph, the mistake with learning group scored approximately 3.1, the control group approximately 2.9, and the mistake without learning group approximately 2.6.

The learning-based group demonstrates the highest average humility, directly supporting the hypothesis.

The remaining statements either give a true but unrelated comparison, misrepresent the graph, or make a claim that cannot be verified from the graph.

That confirms A is correct.
